name: Milkfish
description: The most important cultured fish in the Philippines, with Pampanga and Bulacan being major producers.
location: Pampanga, Bulacan, Cebu, Bohol, Leyte, Samar

name: Rabbitfish
description: Iconic dried fish from Cebu, especially around Mactan. Central to local breakfasts and coastal livelihood.

name: Short-bodied Mackerel
description: Caught in the waters off Leyte and Samar but also traded widely in Central Visayas markets, especially Cebu.

name: Mantis Shrimp
description: Known for their lightning-fast strikes, mantis shrimp are found in coral rubble and sandy seafloors. Bohol is one of the few places where they're traditionally caught.

name: Reef Eel
description: A Cebuano delicacy, especially from the town of Cordova. These eels are grilled or stewed and are part of traditional coastal diets.

name: Mullet
description: Found in the coastal shallows and estuaries of Cebu and Siquijor. These bottom feeders are adapted to brackish and even freshwater systems.

name: Goby
description: A small, often colorful reef and tide pool fish commonly found around Siquijor and Bohol. Many species have unique adhesive pelvic fins.

name: Giant Tiger Prawn
description: Farmed in the mangrove areas of Bohol and southern Cebu. Considered a top-tier seafood product from the region.

name: Giant Trevally
description: Though more known in Batangas, juvenile GTs also enter brackish zones in Negros Oriental, making them a prized catch among local anglers.

name: Skipjack
description: Caught in the deep waters between Bohol and Cebu, especially near the Bohol Sea.

name: Harlequin Shrimp
description: Beautiful shrimp that feeds exclusively on starfish; noted for its vibrant colors.

name: Longhorn Cowfish
description: Box-shaped fish with horn-like projections; emits toxins when threatened.

name: Batfish
description: Laterally flattened fish found near mangroves and reefs.

name: Spiny Seahorse
description: A small seahorse with prickly spines; clings to seagrass.

name: Cleaner Wrasse
description: Known for picking parasites off larger fish.

name: Pipehorse
description: A relative of seahorses and pipefish; long and thin.

name: Porcupinefish
description: Inflates and exposes sharp spines when threatened.

name: Blue Tang
description: Recognizable as “Dory” from movies; grazes on algae.

name: Snowflake Moray Eel
description: Colorful eel with a spotted pattern; hides in reef crevices.

name: Banded Coral Shrimp
description: Red-and-white banded shrimp that cleans parasites from fish.

name: Saddleback Anemonefish
description: Lives in symbiosis with sea anemones; has a saddle-like white patch.

name: Golden Rabbitfish
description: Herbivorous fish often seen in pairs; important in reef health.

name: Coral Catshark
description: Small shark with spotted body; found near coral reefs.

name: Redtoothed Triggerfish
description: Displays striking blue color and red teeth; often seen in schools.

name: Lizardfish
description: Bottom-dweller that ambushes prey; sand-colored for camouflage.

name: Yellowtail Fusilier
description: Small, yellow-tailed schooling fish; important in local markets.

name: Threadfin Bream
description: Delicate pinkish fish caught by demersal trawling.

name: Black Triggerfish
description: Aggressive reef fish with tough body and trigger spine.

name: Bubble Coral Shrimp
description: Transparent shrimp that lives among bubble coral tentacles.

name: Yellowhead Jawfish
description: Lives in sandy burrows; holds eggs in its mouth.

name: Purple Sea Urchin
description: Spiny echinoderm that grazes algae and provides reef aeration.

name: Christmas Tree Worm
description: Colorful, spiral-shaped worm that retracts into coral when disturbed.

name: Saddleback Toby
description: Small pufferfish with a black saddle marking.

name: Coral Grouper
description: Spotted reef predator targeted by spearfishing

name: Spotfin Lionfish
description: Venomous fish with flamboyant pectoral fins.

name: Threadfin Butterflyfish
description: Delicate reef fish with trailing dorsal filament.

name: Pink Anemonefish
description: Pale pink clownfish with white head stripe.

name: Basket Star
description: Nocturnal brittle star with coiling arms; filter feeder.

name: Flame Angelfish
description: Bright red-orange dwarf angelfish with vertical stripes.

name: Broadclub Cuttlefish
description: Large cuttlefish known for dramatic color changes.
location: Seagrass and reef flats of Negros Oriental
